scene: Premium dark gym squat rack, between sets, loading plates.

character_a.name: Dark-Haired Fit Gym Friend
character_a.role: disciplined friend with the simple system

character_b.name: Blond Strength Gym Friend
character_b.role: relatable stressed friend looking for a way to stay consistent

lines.speaker: character_b
lines.text: Work is nonstop. Budget cuts, boss on me... I'm barely keeping up.
lines.shot: Character B medium close-up at squat rack, plate low in frame, mouth clear.

lines.speaker: character_b
lines.text: How do you stay this consistent? You're busier than me.
lines.shot: Character B tighter shot, looks from bar back to Character A, stressed but grounded.

lines.speaker: character_a
lines.text: Meal Prep Revolution. Best switch I've made.
lines.shot: Character A reverse shot, calm answer while setting up for next set.

lines.speaker: character_a
lines.text: I pick the week's meals online, check out, and they show up at my door.
lines.shot: Character A close-up, plate movement stays below chest, slight nod on delivery.

lines.speaker: character_a
lines.text: No shopping, cooking, or cleanup. I heat one up and get back to work.
lines.shot: Character A reverse angle with rack depth, relaxed practical delivery.

lines.speaker: character_a
lines.text: Clean ingredients, macro-friendly, keeps me on track.
lines.shot: Character A clean hero headshot, mouth visible, confident not salesy.

lines.speaker: character_b
lines.shot: Character B reaction shot, impressed and practical, hand moving toward phone optional.

lines.speaker: character_a_vo
lines.text: MealPrepRevolution.com. Pick your meals. Own your week.
lines.shot: Fade from gym into uploaded Meal Prep Revolution website screenshot; overlay CTA.

lip_sync_rules: Create one TTS node per line before video generation.
Pass the exact TTS audio into the matching lip-sync/video node.
Only one visible speaking mouth per shot.
Keep hands, plates, bars, and rack uprights away from the mouth.
Use close-up or medium close-up headshots for all spoken lines.
Leave 150-250 ms edit handles between lines.
If lip sync drifts, reduce body motion and regenerate as a calmer headshot.
draft_efficiency_rules: Use the lowest practical resolution available, preferably 720p or lower.
Use the cheapest acceptable lip-sync/video model for the first wiring proof.
Generate one line at a time so failed lines can be replaced without rerendering the full ad.
Do not upscale, extend, interpolate, color-grade, or final-master draft shots.
Keep movement simple: mouth visible, head stable, plate/rack motion below the mouth line.
Use shared character locks and prompt bases instead of restating full identity details in every node.
Do not run premium/professional final generation until the low-res assembly is approved.
compliance_note: Do not use a precise 20-hours-per-week savings claim unless substantiated.
